storyline:
GEO + MSTR — the 2024 US Election concurrency test: one catalyst (Trump's Nov-5 win) spawning several thesis-driven plays. Two are seeded from real coverage — an immigration crackdown (GEO, the largest private ICE-detention operator, +42% the session after the win) and a crypto-friendly agenda (MSTR, the leveraged Bitcoin proxy) — plus a co-discovered nuclear / energy cluster the matcher collapsed into one basket (CEG + CCJ + peers). The MSTR result is the story: reframing its 2-month pre-election run as anticipation of the dated Nov-5 election (a resolvable event whose exit is the vote itself) — not 'Bitcoin momentum' — let MSTR enter Sept-20, ride the run (+59% pick), and exit cleanly at the election when the catalyst resolved. That clean exit is the point: it gives MSTR's otherwise open-ended momentum thesis a real resolution, dodging the ~45%-crash ride a dateless hold would take. MSTR is now the dominant winner (ev2, +$52k). It required a surgical loosening of the scout's catalyst gate to admit dated-future-event anticipation (an election / FDA date / scheduled vote) while still rejecting dateless momentum — a prototype not yet scoreboard-gated across the other gems. Net +75%, a noisy single draw: the nuclear basket (ev1) dragged −$18k as the AI-power theme cooled and GEO+CoreCivic (ev3) slipped −$6k; the gold floor + MSTR carried it.
This gem’s event-agent (ev3) — catalyst: Trump win spurs mass-deportation crackdown, ICE detention contracts expand. Exit: still live — none - no policy reversal, curbs lifted, or contract cancellations; driver structurally intact though quiet.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • defensive · GLD — catalyst: always-on defensive (GLD) floor agent. Exit: still live (catalyst active).
  • ev1 · CCJ, CEG, OKLO, URA, VST — catalyst: PJM capacity auction prices jumped ninefold on grid shortage. Exit: still live — none - PJM shortage structural driver unreversed; no capacity glut, re-regulation rollback, or auction retraction has occurred.
  • ev2 · MSTR — catalyst: Bitcoin rallies as pro-crypto Trump's Nov-5 election odds rise. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2024-11-08 — Nov-5 election has occurred and resolved with Trump's win - the specific catalyst is now public and priced.

seeds.title: Bitcoin and MicroStrategy rally as pro-crypto Trump climbs in the polls ahead of the Nov-5 election
seeds.snippet: Bitcoin and MicroStrategy (MSTR) are climbing as prediction markets price rising odds that pro-crypto candidate Donald Trump wins the Nov-5 US presidential election - an outcome that would establish a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and ease crypto regulation. MSTR, the leveraged Bitcoin proxy, is the purest equity way to own the election-anticipation trade; the catalyst resolves on election day Nov 5. Still under-owned as the run to the vote is only beginning.

seeds.title: Bitcoin and MicroStrategy extend gains as Trump-win odds firm into the Nov-5 vote
seeds.snippet: Bitcoin and MicroStrategy (MSTR) extended their climb as betting markets moved further toward a Trump win in the Nov-5 US presidential election - a pro-crypto outcome that has pledged a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and lighter regulation. MSTR, the leveraged Bitcoin proxy, rises with the anticipated result; the catalyst is the discrete Nov-5 vote that resolves the trade. Still under-owned relative to the binary event just weeks ahead.

seeds.title: MicroStrategy unveils a $42 billion '21/21' plan to buy Bitcoin - days before the pro-crypto election it is positioned for
seeds.snippet: MicroStrategy announced a $42 billion capital plan on Oct 30 - $21 billion of equity plus $21 billion of fixed-income over three years - to expand its Bitcoin holdings, the largest corporate Bitcoin-accumulation program ever, timed days before the Nov-5 election a pro-crypto Trump win would supercharge. MSTR is the leveraged proxy on the anticipated election outcome; the discrete catalyst is the Nov-5 vote, which resolves the anticipation trade.

seeds.title: Trump's win sends private-prison stocks soaring; GEO Group the purest ICE-detention play, still under-owned
seeds.snippet: Shares of private-prison operators soared after Donald Trump's election win on expectations of a hard immigration crackdown and mass deportations. GEO Group jumped about 42% in a single session and CoreCivic 29%. ICE contracts already account for roughly 43% of GEO's revenue, and it is the largest private operator of ICE detention capacity - positioned to win the detention-bed buildout a mass-deportation surge requires. Still under-owned as the enforcement ramp is only beginning.

seeds.title: Pro-crypto Trump wins the Nov-5 election; the MicroStrategy anticipation trade resolves as Bitcoin hits records
seeds.snippet: The Nov-5 election resolved with a win for pro-crypto candidate Donald Trump, who has pledged a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and crypto deregulation. Bitcoin hit records and MicroStrategy (MSTR), the leveraged proxy that rode the anticipation into the vote, jumped more than 50% since Nov 5 - the discrete election catalyst has now occurred, turning the trade from anticipation to a bet on actual policy enactment.

seeds.title: Trump names Tom Homan 'border czar'; GEO Group climbs again as deportation machinery takes shape
seeds.snippet: Private-prison stocks rose again after President-elect Trump named Tom Homan - who ran immigration enforcement in his first term - as his 'border czar,' signaling the deportation apparatus is being staffed immediately. GEO Group, the largest private ICE-detention contractor, is the most direct beneficiary of the coming detention-capacity expansion; the enforcement buildout is only just starting and the vehicle remains under-owned relative to the contracted bed pipeline.

CEG.thesis: PJM capacity auction prices jumped ninefold on grid shortage
CEG.exit_case: none
CEG.assessment: PJM's ninefold capacity price jump (to ~$269/MW-day) confirms a structural grid shortage driven by surging data-center/AI power demand outpacing supply, especially from retiring baseload plants. This is day one of the thesis - the auction result is fresh, under-the-radar relative to AI-demand narratives, and directly monetizable via merchant nuclear/generation names with capacity exposure in PJM territory. No reversal in sight; if anything, this sets up a multi-year re-rating as future auctions and potential re-regulation debates unfold.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold CEG and VST; PJM capacity crunch is structural (data-center demand, retiring baseload) with next capacity auction and PJM/FERC policy responses still ahead as concrete milestones.
